def: s.m. A chain, a fetter; a chain worn round the waist, a belt (vulg. sāṅkal).
s.m. = H سرنکهلا सृंखला sr̤iṅkhalā, s.f. See śr̤iṅkhal and śr̤iṅkhalā.
s.f. = H سرنکهل सृंखल sr̤iṅkhal, s.m. See śr̤iṅkhal and śr̤iṅkhalā.
s.f. = next, q.v.
part. (f. ā), Created, produced, made; — put or sent away, deserted, abandoned; attached, joined, connected ornamented, adorned; — abundant, much, many.
s.f. Creating; creation, production, invention; the creation, the world; nature; natural disposition; natural state; natural property; — a liberal gift
subs: s.m. lit. 'The wheel of creation,' the entire system of created things, the universe
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit सृष्टि (sṛṣṭi). Doublet of سِٹّھِی (siṭṭhī).

def: s.m. The ear.
s.m. corr. of srot, q.v.
s.m. = srotas, and sotā, qq.v. s.m. Flow or course of water, current; a rapid stream, a river, a torrent; a wave; a spring (cf. sot; sotā).
s.m. (f. -trī), A hearer, listener; a pupil.
s.m. One who hears, hearer.
s.m. See srotas, and sotā.
s.m. = شروت śrota, q.v. s.m. See srotas, and sotā.
s.m. Flow or course of water, current; a rapid stream, a river, a torrent; a wave; a spring (cf. sot; sotā).
s.m. Flow or course of water, current; a rapid stream, a river, a torrent; a wave; a spring (cf. sot; sotā).
part. adj. To be heard, audible; fit or proper to be heard, to be listened to.
s.m. A Brāhman versed in the study of the Vedas.
part. = شرت śruta, q.v.

part. (of śru), Heard, listened to; heard of, reported, rumoured, said; ascertained, understood; called; — s.m. That which was heard from the beginning, the Veda, revelation, holy writ, sacred science or learning
subs: s.f. Name of a daughter of Sūr, and wife of Vriddha-śarman
s.f. 'Goddess of learning,' epithet of Sarasvatī.

def: s.f. = شرتي śruti, q.v.

s.f. The sense of hearing; the ear; anything heard, sound, report, rumour, intelligence; that which was heard or revealed from the beginning, revelation, revealed law, holy writ, the Veda; any Vedic or sacred text; (in Geom.) the diagonal of a tetragon; the hypothenuse of a right-angled triangle; (in Music) a particular division of the octave, a quarter tone or interval (of which twenty-two are enumerated, four constituting a major tone, three a minor, and two a semi-tone)
subs: s.m. The essence or substance of the Vedas
s.m. 'One who traffics in the Vedas,' an epithet of any Brāhmaṇ.

def: s.f. A sacrificial ladle ( = sruv, or surwā); — the Mūrvā plant; the tree Boswellia thurifera; the plants Sansviera Zeylanica; Aletris hyacinthoides and Dracona nervosa.
s.m. (f. -ā), See surwā.
part. adj. Fixed, firm, stiff, rigid, hard, immovable, motionless, numb, paralysed, insensible; senseless, stupid, dull; stopped, blocked, shut up, brought to a stand.
s.m. Post, pillar, column, pile; stem; hindrance, obstruction; stupefaction, insensibility; stupidity; coldness, numbness, paralysis.
s.m. Post, pillar, column, pile; stem; hindrance, obstruction; stupefaction, insensibility; stupidity; coldness, numbness, paralysis.
s.m. = S ستنبهن स्तंभन stambhan, s.m. See ستمبهہ stambh, and ستمبهن stambhan.

s.m. Propping, supporting, holding upright; stopping, bringing to a stand, blocking up, hindering, obstructing, obstruction; paralyzing; benumbing; stunning, stupefying, stupefaction, insensibility; suppressing the use of the faculties by magic or supernatural means; — an astringent.
s.m. = S ستنبهہ स्तंभ stambh, s.m. See ستمبهہ stambh, and ستمبهن stambhan.
part. Proppea, supported; stopped, brought to a stand-still; benumbed, paralyzed; — stupefied, senseless.

ety: Pehl. śtān; Zend śtāna; S. स्थान
def: s.m. Place, situation, station, stall (used as an affix to nouns ending in a vowel, e.g. bo-stān, 'place of fragrance,' 'flower-garden'; — hindū-stān, India: if the subst. terminate in a consonant, the affix takes the form istān, e.g. reg-istān, 'place of sand,' 'a desert'; — gul-istān, 'place of roses,' 'rose-garden').
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ـِسْتَان (-istān), from Middle Persian -stʾn' (-estān), from Old Persian 𐎿𐎫𐎠𐎴 (s-t-a-n /⁠stāna⁠/), from Proto-Iranian *stā́nam, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *stʰáHnam, from Proto-Indo-European *steh₂- (“to stand”). Doublet of تھانَہ (thāna) and سْتھان (sthān).

def: s.m. Breast (of a woman); nipple; teat, dug.
s.m. Praising, celebrating, hymning; praise, eulogium, encomium, panegyric; flattery (cf. stuti).
adj. & s.m. Praising, eulogizing; — praiser, panegyrist, encomiast; flatterer.
s.m. Thief, robber; — thieving, stealing.
s.m. A station (civil or military); railway-station.
s.m. Theft, robbery; anything stolen.
wety: From Sanskrit सत्य (satya, “true; truth, reality”).

def: part. Covered; — tired, weary (syn. thakā, q.v.).
s.m. Firmness, stability, fixedness, steadiness; continuance; steadfastness, constancy; fortitude; patience; calmness; resoluteness.
s.m. Firmness, stability, fixedness, steadiness; continuance; steadfastness, constancy; fortitude; patience; calmness; resoluteness.

s.m. Firm ground, dry land; place, site, spot, region, soil; standing-place of a faqīr; home, abode, fixed residence; a mound; terrace; a tent; part or place (in a book); a point, topic; case, occasion, circumstance
subs: s.m. or sthal-padminī, s.f. = sthal-kamal, q.v.
vulg. sthalaj, adj. (f. -ā), Land-born, produced on land, growing on earth; terrestrial, terrene, not aquatic
s.m. or sthal-kamalinī, s.f. The flowering shrub Hibiscus mutabilis.

def: s.f. = sthal, q.v. s.m. Firm ground, dry land; place, site, spot, region, soil; standing-place of a faqīr; home, abode, fixed residence; a mound; terrace; a tent; part or place (in a book); a point, topic; case, occasion, circumstance
subs: s.m. or sthal-padminī, s.f. = sthal-kamal, q.v.
vulg. sthalaj, adj. (f. -ā), Land-born, produced on land, growing on earth; terrestrial, terrene, not aquatic
s.m. or sthal-kamalinī, s.f. The flowering shrub Hibiscus mutabilis.

def: s.f. A small flat metal or earthen dish or pan, a boiler, cooking-pan; platter, tray, salver (vulg thālī).

s.f. = sthal, q.v. s.m. Firm ground, dry land; place, site, spot, region, soil; standing-place of a faqīr; home, abode, fixed residence; a mound; terrace; a tent; part or place (in a book); a point, topic; case, occasion, circumstance
subs: s.m. or sthal-padminī, s.f. = sthal-kamal, q.v.
vulg. sthalaj, adj. (f. -ā), Land-born, produced on land, growing on earth; terrestrial, terrene, not aquatic
s.m. or sthal-kamalinī, s.f. The flowering shrub Hibiscus mutabilis.

def: s.m. The act of standing or staying, stay, continuance; place, spot, locality, situation, site, space, station, stall, abode, house, dwelling, residence, home; occasion; object; place, section, or chapter (in a book); station, degree, rank, dignity; appointment, office
subs: (in Gram.) Having reference to place; an adverb of place.
ex: اس کا گفٹ ستھان میں ہے۔
us kā gifṭ sthān mẽ hai.
His gift is at a location.
یہاں تمہرے لیئے ستھان نیہں ہے۔
yahā̃ tumhe līe sthān nahī̃ hai.
There is no room for you here.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit स्थान (sthāna). Compare with Avestan 𐬯𐬙𐬁𐬥𐬀- (stāna-) and Persian ـستان (-estân). Doublet of ـستان (-(i)stān), تھان (thān), and تھانَہ (thāna).

def: adj. Having place or situation, having fixedness, fixed, placed; abiding, permanent; resident.
s.m. Placing, fixing; establishing, confirming; setting up, erecting, founding, instituting, regulating, appointing
subs: (-kā), To set up, erect; to establish, found; to appoint, &c.

def: s.f. Placing, &c. ( = sthāpan, q.v.); arranging (as a drama, &c.); stage-management.
s.f. The plant Cissampelos heæandra.
part. Fixed, established, placed; set up, erected; founded, instituted, endowed, set apart; ordered, regulated, decreed, enacted, enjoined; settled, ascertained.

ety: sthāp˚ = S. स्थापय(ति), caus. of rt. स्था
def: v.t. To place, fix, locate; to establish, &c. (see sthāpan).

s.m. = S ستهولتا स्थूलता sthūlatā, s.f. = S ستهولتو स्थूलत्व sthūlatva, s.m. Bigness, stoutness, bulkiness, corpulence; bulk, size; thickness, grossness, coarseness, denseness, dulness, doltishness, stupidity.

adj. & s.m. Standing still, fixed, stationary; firm, stable, immovable; established, regular; inert, inactive, slow; — a stationary or inanimate object; a mountain; an old or fixed resident (of a place), a fixture, a stay-at-home; — immovable property, real estate (such as land or houses); heir-loom, family possession; a bow-string
adj. Standing, staying, residing, abiding, continuing, enduring, permanent, constant, fixed, steady, firm, unchangeable
subs: s.m. Permanent or fixed condition; lasting feeling.

def: adj. Disposed to stay, likely to last, abiding, staying, stopping, stationary, steady, firm; — s.m. The overseer of a village.

adj. Fixed, immovable, firm, steady, unfluctuating, permanent, enduring, durable, lasting, settled; steadfast, constant, faithful; determined; still, calm, mild, tranquil, serene; cool, collected, composed; quiet, quiescent; — s.m. A deity, an immortal; an epithet of Śiva, and of Kārttikeya (the god of war); final emancipation from existence; a mountain; a tree; a bull; the planet Saturn; name of one of the astronomical yogas
subs: (˚ra+āt˚), Firm-minded, firm, unmoved, resolute, steady, stable
sthir-ćetā, adj. Firm-minded, firm, resolute, determined; constant, steady, sedate
adj. Firm-minded, firm, steady, resolute, deliberate, unwavering
adj. Constant of habit, steady, persistent, persevering, &c.
v.n. To be firm; to be at rest, &c.

def: s.f. Firmness, stability, steadiness, permanence, perpetuity; steadfastness, rectitute, fortitude; calmness, coolness, composure, serenity; stillness, rest, inertia.
s.m. = H ستهرتائي स्थिरताई sthirtāʼī, vulg.sithartāʼī, s.f. = sthiratā, q.v.
s.f. = S ستهرتو स्थिरत्व sthiratva, s.m. = sthiratā, q.v.

part. Stood, stayed, remained, stopped; standing; abiding, dwelling, residing, resident; fixed, permanent, established, settled, steady, firm, immovable, stable, steadfast, determined, resolved; decreed
subs: (-par), To stand (upon); to be steadfast (to), to be resolved (on), &c.

def: s.f. The being fixed or stationary; standing, staying, remaining, abiding; stay, residence; steadiness, continuance, permanence; preservation; stability, duration, fixedness; consistency; stop, cessation; pause; decision, decree; maxim.
adj. Great, large, bulky, fat, corpulent, unwieldy; gross, coarse; clumsy, dull, stupid; — s.m. A heap; a tent; — the jack-tree
subs: adj. Crass of intellect, dull-witted, thick-headed, stupid, dull, senseless; coarse-minded
adj. & s.m. Dull of perception, dull, obtuse, stupid; — dolt, blockhead, fool.

def: s.f. = S ستهولتو स्थूलत्व sthūlatva, s.m. = S ستهوليه स्थौल्य sthaulya, s.m. Bigness, stoutness, bulkiness, corpulence; bulk, size; thickness, grossness, coarseness, denseness, dulness, doltishness, stupidity.

s.m. = S ستهولتا स्थूलता sthūlatā, s.f. = S ستهوليه स्थौल्य sthaulya, s.m. Bigness, stoutness, bulkiness, corpulence; bulk, size; thickness, grossness, coarseness, denseness, dulness, doltishness, stupidity.
s.f. Post, pillar, column, prop ( = sutūn); an iron image, a statue; an anvil; — a disease.
s.m. Praiser, encomiast, panegyrist; flatterer (syn. stut-kār).
s.m. Praise, eulogy; hymn of praise; ode.
s.m. = stotā, q.v. s.m. Praiser, encomiast, panegyrist; flatterer (syn. stut-kār).
adj. Of or belonging to women; suited to women; female, feminine; — hen-pecked; — s.m. The nature of woman; womanhood, feminineness; the female sex.
s.f. A woman; a wife
subs: adj. (in Gram.) Of the feminine gender, feminine
adj. & s.m. Wife-subdued, hen-pecked; — a man ruled by his wife, a hen-pecked husband
s.m. The duty of a woman or wife, female duties; the laws concerning women
s.m. The kingdom of women (a country placed by some in the region of Bhoṭan)
s.m. A jewel or gem of a woman, an excellent woman; an epithet of Lakshmī
adj. Of the form of a woman, female, feminine
To take a wife, to marry; to take to oneself a woman (as a concubine)
s.m. The female organ; (in Gram.) the feminine gender
s.m. The female sex, woman-kind, women-folk
s.m. Subjection to women; submissiveness to a wife; — adj. Hen-pecked
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit स्त्री (strī́).

def: s.f. = S ستريتو स्त्रीत्व strītva, s.m. Womanhood; wife-hood; womanliness; feminineness, effeminacy.
s.m. = S ستريتا स्त्रीता strītā, s.f. Womanhood; wife-hood; womanliness; feminineness, effeminacy.
part. adj. Praising, celebrating (used as last member of compounds, e.g. deva-stut, 'praising the gods').
s.f. contrac. of ستتي stuti, q.v.
part. Praised, eulogized, hymned, glorified; flattered.
s.f. Praise, laudation, magnifying, eulogy, commendation; adulation, flattery; — a hymn of praise, hymn, psalm, anthem, canticle
subs: (-kī), To sing the praises (of); to hymn

def: s.f. Side, part, quarter, direction; — prep. Towards, in the direction (of)
subs: adv. Side by side; on every side.
wety: Perhaps from Sanskrit सह (saha).

def: insep. prefix [opp. to dus or dur, and ku; and corresponding to the Gr. εὖ), Good, well, excellent, excellently: neat, elegant, beautiful, beautifully; honourable, worthy of respect or reverence; excessive, excessively, exceedingly, much, very; readily, easily, willingly, quickly. (It is a particle which is prefixed to substantives almost ad libitum. Many words beginning with it will be found in Hindī literature which it has been deemed superfluous to insert in a dictionary.)
wety: Perhaps from Sanskrit सह (saha).

def: postpn. (Braj.) = se, and soṅ, qq.v. The sixth letter of the Hindūstānī alphabet (the fourth of the Arabic) occurs only in words borrowed from the Arabic. Its sound in Arabic is that of th in the English thin; but in Hindūstānī it has the sound of s. According to the abjad, q.v., it has the numerical value of 500.
wety: Perhaps from Sanskrit सह (saha).

ety: for sūʼuṉ, v.n. fr. سوأ 'to be evil,' &c.
def: s.m. Evilness, badness, foulness, unseemliness (used, in Urdū, only in comp.)
wety: Perhaps from Sanskrit सह (saha).

def: adj. = sūhā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Red, crimson; saffron-coloured (also written sūʼā); — cloth dyed saffron-colour; — name of a rāg or musical mode
subs: adj. Red as the dye of saffron.

def: s.m. A parrot; paroquet ( = suggā or sūgā).

ety: prob. S. सूच+कः; see sūʼī, and cf. next
def: s.m. A large needle, a packing needle.
s.m. Awn (of barley, &c.); a bristle; hair; spicule; prickle
subs: s.f. The game of hazard played by drawing lines on the ground); — sūʼā-pokā, s.m. A kind of caterpillar covered with hairs
s.m. (local) The jamblong with an awl-shaped fruit, Calyptranthes caryophyllifolia.

def: s.m. Asking, questioning; solicitation (of alms), begging; question, query, interrogation, interrogatory; demand, request, application, petition; a problem
subs: s.m. An indicatory or leading question
s.m. Question and answer; dialogue; discussion, debate; cross-examination
To debate, to argue; to cross-examine; to plead, advocate; to transact, negotiate
To solve a problem, work out a question
s.f. Hearing an application, entertaining a case or suit
lit. 'Question within or upon question,' cross-examination
prov. lit. 'The question another (or one thing), the answer another'; cross purposes and crooked answers
(-ko), To set (one) a question or problem; to make an application (to); to present a petition (to), to petition
(-meṅ), To put (in) a question, to question, ask; to request, beg
(-se), To ask (of), to question, interrogate; to examine (a pupil, or witness, &c.); to solicit (alms), to beg
s.m. A guiding or leading question.

r: suʼālāt, suwālāt, sawālāt, suʼāl, suʼālāt-ě-ibtidāʼī, suʼālāt-ě-imtiḥān, suʼālāt-ě-taḥrīrī, suʼālāt-ě-ḥal-t̤alab, suʼālāt-ě-zabānī, suʼālāt-ě-ʻilmī, suʼālāt-ke kāg̠aẓ
def: s.m. pl. (of suʼāl, q.v.), Questions; demands; — suʼālāt-ě-ibtidāʼī, Preliminary questions
s.m. Questioner; — the person who sings the mars̤iya (q.v.).
s.m. An alloy of gold and copper.
s.f. See s.v. sūʼā. s.m. A parrot; paroquet ( = suggā or sūgā).
s.m. (f. -ī), A hog, pig; a term of abuse (cf. Fr. cochon)
subs: s.m. A pig-sty; a pork-establishment, any place where swine are slaughtered or sold
'Boar's onset,' a desperate onset or attack.

def: s.f. A sow ( = sūʼarnī.
s.f. A sow (see sūʼar).
s.m. A kind of rice (lit. 'sweet-smelling').
s.m. Seventh part, a seventh.

ety: for A. صوبة 'a collection'; v.n. fr. صوب
def: s.m. The chief, governor, viceroy, or lieutenant of a province; — a native military officer (among Indian troops) whose rank corresponds to that of captain
subs: vulg. ṣūbe-dārī, adj. Of or relating to a ṣūba-dār; proceeding from or issued by a ṣūba-dār; — s.f. The office of lieutenant of a province, viceroyalty; the government of a ṣūba or province; — the rank of a ṣūba-dār or captain.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صوبه (sūba)

def: s.m. corr. of صوبه ṣūba, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Handsome-armed; strong-armed; — an epithet of Rāvan (cf. su-bhuj).
s.m. pl. (of ṣūba, after the model of the Ar., the final h silent being changed to j), Provinces.
v.n. To sob ( = sisaknā).
s.m. = suvaktā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Speaking elegantly or well, eloquent; — an elegant or eloquent speaker.
adj. (f. -ā), Well bound or secured, well fastened, having a good binding.
adj. & s.m. Having a good friend; having good connections; — a good friend
s.f. Sweet voice, pleasant sound or speech.

ety: Prk. सुब्बडो, and सुब्बडओ; S. शुल्ब+र, and र+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Alloyed, impure (silver, &c.); — alloyed silver or gold.
adj. & s.m. = subaran, and su-varṇ, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Of a bright colour; golden; — gold, &c. (see su-varṇ).
adj. Sweet-smelling, fragrant; — sweet smell, odour, fragrance, perfume.
s.m. A pleasant dwelling; a good abode
subs: v.n. To live in a pleasant dwelling; to prosper, thrive.

def: s.m. = su-vās, q.v. s.m. A pleasant dwelling; a reputable residence; — an agreeable perfume, sweet smell, fragrance, scent; — adj. Sweet-smelling, odorous, fragrant.
s.m. corr. of su-bhaṭṭa, q.v. s.m. A very learned man; — a distiuguished warrior, a hero, champion.

ety: for A. سبة, v.n. fr. سبّ
def: s.f. A thing that occasions one's being reviled, reproach, disgrace, shame; slander; injury; villainy.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَبْت (sabt), from Arabic اَلسَّبْت (as-sabt).

def: s.m. (colloq.) = su-vesh, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = شبهہ śubh, q.v.
s.f. Dawn, daybreak, morning
subs: or ṣubḥ-ḵẖezān, part. adj. Rising early in the morning
or ṣubḥ-ḵẖezā, or ṣubḥ-ḵẖeziyā, s.m. A thief who rises early in the morning and steals before people are awake
s.f. 'The true dawn' (opp. to ṣubḥ-ě-kāẕib), dawn of day
s.f. The false dawn, the time just before daybreak
v.n. To pass the whole night till dawn
or ṣubḥ-gāhāṅ, s.f. The morning time, the early dawn
adj. Of or relating to the morning or the early dawn
v.t. To put off from time to time; to evade, to shilly-shally
v.n. To dawn, to become morning.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صُبْح (subh), from Arabic صُبْح (ṣubḥ).

def: s.m. corr. of شبہا shubhā, q.v. s.m. Confusedness, dubiousness, doubt, uncertainty, ambiguity; hesitation, suspicion; suspense; question, scruple; a defect (esp. in law), a flaw; presumption (of guilt)
subs: (-kā), To entertain doubt (of), to doubt; to suspect, distrust
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit सभा (sabhā).

def: s.m. corr. of shubha, q.v. s.m. Confusedness, dubiousness, doubt, uncertainty, ambiguity; hesitation, suspicion; suspense; question, scruple; a defect (esp. in law), a flaw; presumption (of guilt)
subs: (-kā), To entertain doubt (of), to doubt; to suspect, distrust

def: adj. (f. -ā), Bright, shining, splendid; handsome, beautiful; auspicious, fortunate, lucky, happy; well, right, good, virtuous; eminent, distinguished; learned, versed (esp. in the Vedas); — s.m. Anything bright or beautiful; auspiciousness, good fortune, happiness, good, welfare, prosperity; — name of one of the astrological yogas
subs: (˚bha+āć˚), adj. & s.m. Pure in practices or observances, well-behaved, virtuous; — a well-behaved or virtuous person
(˚bha+aś˚), adj. (f. -ā) & s.m. Prosperous and unfortunate, good and evil, good or wicked; — good and bad fortune, weal and woe
(˚bha+an˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Handsome-limbed, beautifully formed, handsome, elegant; — a handsome person
s.m. Auspicious result, good or happy consequence
s.m. (f. -ā, -ikā, or -ī), One whose thoughts are excellent; a well-wisher, friend
vulg. śubhad, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Granting happiness, propitious, auspicious; — a benefactor
śubh-dāʼī, or śubh-dāyak, adj. & s.m. = śubha-da, q.v.
s.m. An excellent or auspicious gift; — the recipient of an auspicious gift
s.f. The female elephant of the north-west quarter
s.m. A glorious or a pleasing sound
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Having a good disposition, well-disposed, good-natured; — a good-natured person
s.m. A good season or time; a virtuous epoch; a favourable opportunity
s.m. An auspicious action; a good or virtuous act; reputable occupation or employment
vulg. śubhag, adj. Going well or beautifully, graceful, elegant; fortunate, propitious, auspicious
s.m. An auspicious planet, a lucky star
s.m. Excellent quality, virtue; — śubh-guṇ-sadan, s.m. 'The mansion of excellent qualities,' an epithet of Ganeś (and of other deities)
s.m. The rising of an auspicious constellation; a lucky moment
adj. Causing welfare, producing good, conferring happiness or fortune; auspicious, propitious; prosperous, lucky; — s.m. An accountant; arithmetician.

ety: for A. سبحة subḥat, v.n. fr. سبح
def: s.m. A string of beads (gen. ninety-nine in number; used in performing the tasbīḥ, q.v.), a rosary
subs: part. adj. & s.m. Turning or revolving the rosary; one who is counting his beads
s.f. The act of counting the beads.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Propitious; auspicious, fortunate; — s.m. An epithet of Vishṇu.
s.m. = sau-bhāgya, q.v. (and cf. suhāg). s.m. Auspiciousness, good fortune, fortunateness; blessedness; the fourth of the astronomical yogas
subs: saubhāgya-vat, or saubhāgya-vant, adj. (f. -vatī), Possessing good fortune, fortunate, auspicious, blessed
s.f. A married woman whose husband is alive (esp. if she also have children).

def: adj. (f. -ā), Possessing good fortune, very fortunate or prosperous, happy, blessed, highly-favoured, auspicious; beautiful, lovely, charming; agreeable, pleasant, good; neat, pure; beloved, liked, amiable; — s.m. Fortune; — the Aśoka tree; the Champaka tree.
= S شبهنکر शभंकर śubhaṅ-kar, See s.v. śubh.
s.f. A woman beloved by her husband; a favourite wife; an honoured mother of a family.
s.f. Auspiciousness, for tunateness, prosperity; loveliness, agreeableness, amiableness.
adj. Fortunate, auspicious, lucky; well-to-do.
s.m. = saubhāgya, q.v. s.m. Auspiciousness, good fortune, fortunateness; blessedness; the fourth of the astronomical yogas
subs: saubhāgya-vat, or saubhāgya-vant, adj. (f. -vatī), Possessing good fortune, fortunate, auspicious, blessed
s.f. A married woman whose husband is alive (esp. if she also have children).

ety: inf. n. of سبح 'to magnify or glorify God'
def: s.m. Magnifying, glorifying, or praising (God)
subs: Far be it from God! Holy God! Good God! God be praised! wonderful! strange! bravo! well-done!
Glory be to Him; the Most Holy.

ety: subhā˚ = S. शोभय(ति), fr शोभ; — cf. subhāʼonā
def: v.t. To impart grace or elegance (to, -ko); to make beautiful; — to give pleasure (to; — cf. bhānā).
adj. Of God; divine.
= S شبهگ शुभग śubhag, See s.v. śubh.
adj. & s.m. = سبهاو su-bhāv, q.v. s.m. Good disposition or nature; — adj. Of a good disposition, good-natured, well-disposed; of good quality.
s.f. = H سبهاسي सुभासी subhāsī, s.m. see subhāshā, and subhāshī.
s.f. Good language; pleasant speech.
s.m. A fair speaker; an elegant or eloquent speaker.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Well-spoken, well-said; spoken elegantly, eloquent; — s.m. Elegant speech, eloquence.
s.m. = H سبهاسا सुभासा subhāsā, s.f. see subhāshā, and subhāshī.
s.m. A very learned man; — a distiuguished warrior, a hero, champion.
s.m. Good disposition or nature; — adj. Of a good disposition, good-natured, well-disposed; of good quality.
s.m. = سوبهاو svabhāv, q.v.
s.m. = su-bhāw or subhāʼo, q.v. s.m. Good disposition or nature; — adj. Of a good disposition, good-natured, well-disposed; of good quality.

ety: S. शुभं+हित+कः; but cf. S. सुविहितः
def: s.m. Auspicious time or moment, favourable opportunity, convenient season or time, opportunity, time, leisure, convenience; accommodation, time of peace and plenty or of prosperous circumstances; prosperity, security, safety, welfare, comfort, ease.

s.m. = subhitā or subhītā, q.v. s.m. Auspicious time or moment, favourable opportunity, convenient season or time, opportunity, time, leisure, convenience; accommodation, time of peace and plenty or of prosperous circumstances; prosperity, security, safety, welfare, comfort, ease.

s.m. = subhītā, q.v. s.m. Auspicious time or moment, favourable opportunity, convenient season or time, opportunity, time, leisure, convenience; accommodation, time of peace and plenty or of prosperous circumstances; prosperity, security, safety, welfare, comfort, ease.
adj. 1˚ = su-bhru; — 2˚ = śubhra and śubh, qq.v. adj. Having beautiful eye-brows, lovely-browed; — s.f. A woman.
adj. Shining, radiant, bright; white; — s.m. White (the colour); silver; sandal; talc
subs: s.m. The thirty-sixth year of Jupiter's cycle

def: s.f. The Ganges; crystal; bamboo-manna.
s.m. A good brother.
adj. Having beautiful eye-brows, lovely-browed; — s.f. A woman.
adj. & s.m. Having powerful arms, strong-armed; an epithet of Rāvan (syn. su-bāhu).
adj. = su-vihita, q.v.; — 2˚ s.m. = subhītā, q.v. part. Well-placed, well-deposited; well-furnished, well-supplied, well-appointed; well-provided; well-arranged; well-done, well-performed.

s.m. = subhītā, q.v. s.m. Auspicious time or moment, favourable opportunity, convenient season or time, opportunity, time, leisure, convenience; accommodation, time of peace and plenty or of prosperous circumstances; prosperity, security, safety, welfare, comfort, ease.
s.m. = su-vipra, q.v. s.m. (f. -ā), A good Brāhman, an excellent priest.
s.f. = su-virati, q.v. s.f. Good rest or cessation, a good end, fortunate termination.

s.m. = subhītā or subītā, q.v. s.m. Auspicious time or moment, favourable opportunity, convenient season or time, opportunity, time, leisure, convenience; accommodation, time of peace and plenty or of prosperous circumstances; prosperity, security, safety, welfare, comfort, ease.

s.m. = subhītā, q.v. s.m. Auspicious time or moment, favourable opportunity, convenient season or time, opportunity, time, leisure, convenience; accommodation, time of peace and plenty or of prosperous circumstances; prosperity, security, safety, welfare, comfort, ease.

s.m. (dialec.) = subītā or subhīta, q.v. s.m. Auspicious time or moment, favourable opportunity, convenient season or time, opportunity, time, leisure, convenience; accommodation, time of peace and plenty or of prosperous circumstances; prosperity, security, safety, welfare, comfort, ease.
s.f. = subkī, q.v. s.f. Sobbing; a sob.
s.f. = sabkī, q.v.
s.f. Sobbing; a sob.

ety: Prk. सुब्बडो, and सुब्बडओ; S. शुल्ब+र, and र+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Alloyed, impure (silver, &c.); — alloyed silver or gold.
adj. Of good understanding, intelligent, wise, clever, shrewd.
s.f. Good understanding.
s.f. Lightness, levity, frivolousness; contempt; indignity, dishonour, disgrace, insult; littleness; fineness, delicacy (syn. ḵẖiffat).
s.f. pl. (of sabīl), Ways, means; modes of life, manners, institutions.
s.m. Permanence; constancy, firmness, &c. ( = s̤abāt, q.v.); proof, testimony, conviction; — adj. Firm; proved; entire ( = s̤ābit)
wety: Borrowed from Arabic ثُبُوت (ṯubūt), possibly via Classical Persian.

def: adj. Bright, clear, clean, white; cleansed, purified, pure, undefiled, unpolluted; virtuous, upright, honest; — s.m. The hot season, the months of May and June
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi سچ (sac, sacc /⁠sc⁠/) (c. 1635), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀘𑁆𑀘 (sacca), from Sanskrit सत्य (satyá, “promise”).

def: s.f. A piercing; making signs, gesticulation; spying out, seeing, sight.
s.m. A pointed shoot or blade of kuśa grass.

ety: suććā, q.v.+āʼī, as in saććāʼī, q.v.
def: s.f. Cleanness, cleanliness, purity; whiteness; sincerity, straightforwardness; simplicity, guilelessness.
wety: From Sanskrit सत्य (satya).

def: adj. Thoughtful, reflective, attentive, careful, caautious.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Indicating, pointing out, indicative, making known or manifest; what intimates or informs, &c.; betraying, informing (against); — a piercer, borer; a needle; an awl; a pointer out, an indicator; a narrator, teacher, instructor; the manager or chief actor of a company; an informer, a spy, tale-bearer, traducer; a villain, scoundrel; a demon, an imp; a crow; a dog; a cat; a proper name.

ety: fr. S. सु+चकित; or fr. sućak, q.v.
def: v.n. To be astonished, to be startled; to be anxious or thoughtful; to shrink, to start back, to hesitate.
s.m. Graceful gait; good behaviour, propriety of character and deportment; — adj. Walking gracefully; behaving with propriety.
s.m. Right conduct, good behaviour.
adj. Very lovely or beautiful, very agreeable, pleasing, delightful.
adj. = suć, q.v.; — s.f. = sućāʼī, q.v. adj. Bright, clear, clean, white; cleansed, purified, pure, undefiled, unpolluted; virtuous, upright, honest; — s.m. The hot season, the months of May and June
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi سچا (sca /⁠saccā⁠/) (c. 1564), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀘𑁆𑀘 (sacca), from Sanskrit सत्य (sátya).

def: s.m. Agreeable enunciation; euphony.
adj. Attentive, mindful, heedful; conscious, aware; cautious, wary, &c. ( = saćet, q.v.); — composed in mind, peaceful, contente, happy.
adj. = سچيت su-ćet, q.v. adj. Attentive, mindful, heedful; conscious, aware; cautious, wary, &c. ( = saćet, q.v.); — composed in mind, peaceful, contente, happy.
s.f. Attentiveness, attention; circumspection, caution; discreetness.

adj. corr. of sūkshma, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Subtle, minute, atomic, atom-like; little, small; slender, thin, fine, attenuated; delicate, tender; exquisite, refined; nice; sharp, acute; shrill; ingenious; artful, crafty; accurate, correct, exact, precise; — s.m. An atom; fine thread; subtlety; craft, fraud; an epithet of the Supreme Spirit; — the clearing-nut plant
subs: s.f. Sharp wit, acute intellect, mental acumen; — adj. Sharp-witted, witty, acute, shrewd, intelligent, penetrating, ingenious; abstracted
and sūkshma-darśī, adj. & s.m. Sharp-sighted, keen-eyed; of acute discernment; acute, quick, intelligent; — a sharp-sighted person, an acute person
s.m. (in Philos.) 'The atom-like body,' the subtle body which is invested by, and is the archetype or pattern of, the gross material frame.

def: s.f. = sūkshmatā, q.v. s.f. = S سوکشمتو सूक्ष्मत्व sūkshmatva, s.m. Thinness, fineness; minuteness, subtlety; acuteness; shrillness.
adj. & s.m. = suć and śući, qq.v. adj. Bright, clear, clean, white; cleansed, purified, pure, undefiled, unpolluted; virtuous, upright, honest; — s.m. The hot season, the months of May and June

adj. Bright, resplendent, clear, clean; white, cleansed, purified, pure; exempt from passion; free from fault; upright, honest, true; innocent; virtuous, pious; — s.m. White (the colour); purity, purification (by ablution, &c.); innocence, virtue, goodness, holiness; correctness, accuracy; judicial acquittal; — a pure man; a tried and faithful minister, true friend; — oblation to fire at the first feeding of an infant; the sun; the moon; the planet Venus or its regent; — the hot season (i.e. the months Jyeshṭha and Áshāḍha, May-June); — a name of Śiva; of Agni or fire; of a Brāhmaṇ

s.f. Piercing, perforating; a needle ( = sūʼī); sharp point (of anything); vertex (of a triangle); a pyramid, a cone; a triangle formed by producing the sides of a trapezium till they meet; a kind of military array (placing the sharpest and most active soldiers in front), a sharp file or column; indication by gesture, making signs, gesticulation; a particular mode of gesticulation or dancing; index, table of contents (of a book)
subs: s.m. lit. 'Indicatory leaf'; an index, table of contents, catalogue.

def: s.f. Deep or mature thought, due consideration or reflection.
part. adj. Well thought about, well-considered or weighed, well pondered, duly reflected upon, well conceived.
adj. Unoccupied, disengaged, at leisure; at (one's) ease; free from thought or anxiety; thoughtless, careless; — attentive, heedful, careful; occupied
subs: (˚ta+arh), adj. Worthy of attention or care.

def: adj. = سچت su-ćit, q.v. adj. Unoccupied, disengaged, at leisure; at (one's) ease; free from thought or anxiety; thoughtless, careless; — attentive, heedful, careful; occupied
subs: (˚ta+arh), adj. Worthy of attention or care.

def: s.f. = شچتا śućitā, q.v.
part. Pointed out, indicated, intimated, made known; communicated; ascertained, known, understood
subs: v.t. To make known, cause to be understood or conceived, &c.

def: s.f. = S شچتو शुचित्व śućitva, s.m. Purity, brightness, cleanness, clearness, resplendence; whiteness; virtue, holiness; spotlessness, innocence.
s.f. Freedom from care or anxiety, ease; attentiveness, mindfulness, attention, carefulness.
s.m. = S شچتا शुचिता śućitā, s.f. Purity, brightness, cleanness, clearness, resplendence; whiteness; virtue, holiness; spotlessness, innocence.
s.m. See s.v. suć. adj. Bright, clear, clean, white; cleansed, purified, pure, undefiled, unpolluted; virtuous, upright, honest; — s.m. The hot season, the months of May and June

ety: Pārsī śūṭ; rt. Zend śu; cf. af-zūd
def: s.m. Utility, advantage, benefit; profit, gain; interest, usury
subs: s.m. The accumulation of interest so as to double the original debt
adj. Useful, beneficial, advantageous, profitable, lucrative; benefited, profited
(-ko), Advantage to accrue (to), to be benefited, to profit

ety: perf. part. of sūdan; rt. Zend śā = S. शा (शो, श्यति)
def: part. Rubbed; worn; ground; dissolved; — s.m. Powder.
s.m. Headache, megrim; vertigo.
s.f. (?) Power, strength.
s.m. A good present; — adj. Very sincere or upright, very liberal in sacrificial gifts.

s.m. (f. -ā), lit. 'Giving liberally'; a cloud; the sea; a mountain; name of Indra's elephant Airāvata; — name of a particular cow-herd; — name of a poor Brāhmaṇ, who came to Dvārakā to ask Krishṇa's aid, and was raised to wealth by him; — name of a river.

s.m. (f. -ā), lit. 'Giving liberally'; a cloud; the sea; a mountain; name of Indra's elephant Airāvata; — name of a particular cow-herd; — name of a poor Brāhmaṇ, who came to Dvārakā to ask Krishṇa's aid, and was raised to wealth by him; — name of a river.
s.m. A good tooth; — adj. (f. -ī), Having good teeth.
s.f. The female elephant of the north-west quarter.
adj. (f. -ā), Good-looking, beautiful, handsome, pleasing; — s.m. The discus of Vishṇu or Krishṇa; — a vulture; an epithet of Śiva; name of Mount Meru; — the rose-apple, Eugenia jambu
subs: s.m. The discus of Vishṇu or Krishṇa ( = su-darśan).

def: s.m. (f. -ī), A good slave or servant; — name of an ancient Hindū king (at whose court both Vasishṭa and Viśvamitra appear to have acted as family priests).
(su+daśā, qq.v.), Good condition or circumstances; fortunate or happy lot; well-being, prosperity.
adj. = سڐول su-ḍaul, q.v. adj. Well-formed, well-shaped, symmetrical, elegant, graceful, handsome
subs: s.f. Gracefulness of bodily shape or form, handsomeness.

def: adj. Well-formed, well-shaped, symmetrical, elegant, graceful, handsome
subs: s.f. Gracefulness of bodily shape or form, handsomeness.

def: s.f. Excellence of shape or form, elegance, gracefulness, prettiness, handsomeness.

s.m. Good gift, auspicious gift; a special gift given on particular solemn occasions (e.g. a gift to a student at his investiture with the sacred thread, a nuptial present, &c.); — one who makes the above present (e.g. a mother, father, husband, &c.).

ety: for A. سدة suddat, v.n. fr. سدّ 'to stop up, to obstruct'
def: s.m. An obstruction in the nose, coryza; (in Med.) any obstruction in the body; scybala; — a vestibule, porch; threshold.
adv. & postpn. = suddhāṅ, q.v. adv. With, along with, together with, inclusive; — only, merely.

adj. (f. -ā), Pure, clean, purified, cleansed; white, bright; stainless, innocent, guileless, simple, genuine, true, fair, honest; correct, accurate, faultless, blameless, innocent; cleared, acquitted, absolved, free; simple, mere, only, alone; unmixed, unadulterated; uncorrupt, unpolluted; authorized, admitted; very, unequalled
subs: adj. & s.m. Pure, righteous; right, correct; — a virtuous person, &c.
s.m. A virtuous nature or disposition; — adj. Of a virtuous nature or disposition
adj. Of pure and sincere purpose, guileless, unsophisticated, innocent, honest; intelligent.

ety: Prk. सवँद्ध, or सउँद्ध; S. समाधा
def: adv. With, along with, together with, inclusive; — only, merely.
s.f. = śuddhatā, q.v.
s.f. = S شدهتو शुद्धत्व śuddhatva, s.m. Purity, cleanness; innocence; freedom from soil or from defect; correctness, accuracy, faultlessness, &c.
s.m. = S شدهتا शुद्धता śuddhatā, s.f. Purity, cleanness; innocence; freedom from soil or from defect; correctness, accuracy, faultlessness, &c.

s.f. Purity, cleanness, freedom from defilement, holiness, sanctity; purification, expiation, cleansing; correctness, accuracy; rectitude; truth, certainty; clearing, clearance, innocence, acquittal; quittance; retaliation
subs: s.m. List of errata (in a book)
śuddhi-kārī, or śuddhi-kar, adj. Causing purity, purifying, correcting, amending; — s.m. Purifier, cleanser, corrector, &c.

def: s.m. A beautiful or fine country; a limb
subs: (S. loc. sing.), adv. In a beautiful country.

def: = H سديسو सुदेसु su-desu (dialec.) = sudeś, and sudeś-e, qq.v.
(dialec.) = H سديسے सुदेसे su-dese = sudeś, and sudeś-e, qq.v.
s.f. Consciousness, knowledge; sensation, perception; thought; memory, remembrace; attention, notice; care, regard; intelligence
subs: s.f. Consciousness and right understanding; sense; sensation, perception, care, &c. ( = sudh)
or sudh bisarnā, To lose (one's) consciousness, to lose (one's) senses
(-kī), To retain consciousness (of), to give (one's) mind or attention (to), to be thoughtful (of), to bear in mind, to care (for), to regard, to look (after)
(-kī), To take thought (of), to remember; to take care (of), to look (after); to inquire (into); to accommodate
adj. Devoid of consciousness, insensible; senseless; out of (one's) senses, beside oneself.

def: adj. Correct, accurate, sound, right, straight; clean, pure, unpolluted
subs: s.f. Correct understanding or knowledge; discrimination, discretion, prudence; soundness of sense and mind, sanity; self-possession, presence of mind
v.t. To set right, put straight, to rectify.

def: s.f. Direction, direct line; aim ( = sīdh, q.v.). s.f. Straightness, directness; direct line, direction, aim (cf. sūdh); — adj. = sīdhā, q.v.
subs: (-kī), To take the line (of); to take aim (at); to go in a direct line (to); to lay out a line (of road)
adv. In a straight line (with, -kī), in the direction (of).

def: s.f. The beverage of the gods, nectar; ambrosia; the nectar or honey of flowers; juice; water; — whitewash, plaster, mortar; a brick; — lightning; — adj. Comfortable, in easy circumstances, at ease, peaceable
subs: (˚dhā+āk˚), s.m. lit. 'Mine of nectar,' the moon (as the supposed repository of the nectar of the gods); moonlight
vulg. sudhāṅsu (˚dhā+aṅ˚), s.m. lit. 'Nectar-rayed,' the moon ( = sudhākar).

ety: Prk. सुद्धओ; S. शुद्ध+कः, rt. शुध्
def: adj. (f. -ī), Proper, correct, true; upright, straightforward, simple, artless, sincere; straight, direct (cf. sīdhā)
subs: (formative of sūdhā), adv. In a straightforward manner, with simplicity, artlessly, sincerely.

def: s.f. corr. of kshudhā, q.v. s.f. Hunger; appetite
subs: (˚dhā + ār˚) adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Pained with hunger, hungry, hungered; — a very hungry person.

def: s.f. corr. of سدها sudhā, q.v. s.f. The beverage of the gods, nectar; ambrosia; the nectar or honey of flowers; juice; water; — whitewash, plaster, mortar; a brick; — lightning; — adj. Comfortable, in easy circumstances, at ease, peaceable
subs: (˚dhā+āk˚), s.m. lit. 'Mine of nectar,' the moon (as the supposed repository of the nectar of the gods); moonlight
vulg. sudhāṅsu (˚dhā+aṅ˚), s.m. lit. 'Nectar-rayed,' the moon ( = sudhākar).

def: adj. = su-ḍaul, q.v. adj. Well-formed, well-shaped, symmetrical, elegant, graceful, handsome
subs: s.f. Gracefulness of bodily shape or form, handsomeness.

def: s.f. (dialec., or corr.) = sidhāʼī, q.v. s.f. Straightness, directness; straightforwardness, frankness, candour, artlessness, simplicity; goodness, excellence.

ety: sūdhā, q.v. + aʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Propriety, correctness, &c. (see sūdhā).

s.m. See s.v. sudhā. s.f. The beverage of the gods, nectar; ambrosia; the nectar or honey of flowers; juice; water; — whitewash, plaster, mortar; a brick; — lightning; — adj. Comfortable, in easy circumstances, at ease, peaceable
subs: (˚dhā+āk˚), s.m. lit. 'Mine of nectar,' the moon (as the supposed repository of the nectar of the gods); moonlight
vulg. sudhāṅsu (˚dhā+aṅ˚), s.m. lit. 'Nectar-rayed,' the moon ( = sudhākar).

def: v.n. To kindle, to ignite, to burn brightly, to blaze ( = sulagnā).
adj. = su-ḍaul, q.v. adj. Well-formed, well-shaped, symmetrical, elegant, graceful, handsome
subs: s.f. Gracefulness of bodily shape or form, handsomeness.

def: adj. = سڐهال su-ḍhāl, q.v. adj. = su-ḍaul, q.v. adj. Well-formed, well-shaped, symmetrical, elegant, graceful, handsome
subs: s.f. Gracefulness of bodily shape or form, handsomeness.

ety: sudh, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To cause to remember, to put in mind, to remind ( = ćetānā).

s.m. See s.v. sudhā. s.f. The beverage of the gods, nectar; ambrosia; the nectar or honey of flowers; juice; water; — whitewash, plaster, mortar; a brick; — lightning; — adj. Comfortable, in easy circumstances, at ease, peaceable
subs: (˚dhā+āk˚), s.m. lit. 'Mine of nectar,' the moon (as the supposed repository of the nectar of the gods); moonlight
vulg. sudhāṅsu (˚dhā+aṅ˚), s.m. lit. 'Nectar-rayed,' the moon ( = sudhākar).

def: adj. = سڐهال su-ḍhāl, q.v. adj. = su-ḍaul, q.v. adj. Well-formed, well-shaped, symmetrical, elegant, graceful, handsome
subs: s.f. Gracefulness of bodily shape or form, handsomeness.

def: adj. Right, proper, correct, well-behaved.
s.m. = sudhārne-hārā, q.v.s.v. sudhārnā.
adj. Keeping well to duty, attentive to duty; — s.m. A council or assembly of the gods; the hall or palace of Indra (given to Ugrasen by Krishṇ).

ety: sudhār˚ = Prk. सुधार(इ), or सुधारे(इ) = S. सुधारय(ति), caus. of सु+धृ
def: v.t. To form, frame, fashion; to work in; to arrange, to manage, provide for; to regulate, correct, reform, rectify, mend, improve, to adjust, to methodize; to adorn; to polish
subs: or sudhāran-hārā, s.m. Fashioner, framer, worker in; arranger, methodizer; manager, adjuster, adorner, &c.
wety: Derived from سدھار (sudhār), which is from Sanskrit *शुद्धकार (*śuddhakāra, “the act of purifying or removing errors”), from शुद्ध (śuddha) + कार (kāra) (compare Sanskrit शुद्धीकरोति (śuddhīkaroti), from शुद्धीकृ (śuddhīkṛ, “to purify, to remove errors”, root), from शुद्ध (śuddha, adjective) + ईकृ (īkṛ, root form)). Cognate with Punjabi ਸੁਧਾਰਨਾ (sudhārnā) / سُدھارنا (sudhārnā), Nepali सुधार्नु (sudhārnu), Bengali …

def: v.n. = sudharnā, q.v. v.t. To form, frame, fashion; to work in; to arrange, to manage, provide for; to regulate, correct, reform, rectify, mend, improve, to adjust, to methodize; to adorn; to polish
subs: or sudhāran-hārā, s.m. Fashioner, framer, worker in; arranger, methodizer; manager, adjuster, adorner, &c.

def: v.n. To be set right, to be mended, be rectified, be regulated, be corrected; to be correct; to be or seem right or good; to avail, answer, succeed; to improve, mend, come right again.
s.m. = sudhārne-hārā, q.v.s.v. sudhārnā.

adv. See s.v. sūdhā. s.f. The beverage of the gods, nectar; ambrosia; the nectar or honey of flowers; juice; water; — whitewash, plaster, mortar; a brick; — lightning; — adj. Comfortable, in easy circumstances, at ease, peaceable
subs: (˚dhā+āk˚), s.m. lit. 'Mine of nectar,' the moon (as the supposed repository of the nectar of the gods); moonlight
vulg. sudhāṅsu (˚dhā+aṅ˚), s.m. lit. 'Nectar-rayed,' the moon ( = sudhākar).

def: adj. & s.m. Intelligent, wise, learned; — an intelligent or learned man, a panḍit.
adv. (dialec.) = suddhāṅ, q.v. adv. With, along with, together with, inclusive; — only, merely.
s.f. Memory, &c. ( = sudh, q.v.). s.f. Consciousness, knowledge; sensation, perception; thought; memory, remembrace; attention, notice; care, regard; intelligence
subs: s.f. Consciousness and right understanding; sense; sensation, perception, care, &c. ( = sudh)
or sudh bisarnā, To lose (one's) consciousness, to lose (one's) senses
(-kī), To retain consciousness (of), to give (one's) mind or attention (to), to be thoughtful (of), to bear in mind, to care (for), to regard, to look (after)
(-kī), To take thought (of), to remember; to take care (of), to look (after); to inquire (into); to accommodate
adj. Devoid of consciousness, insensible; senseless; out of (one's) senses, beside oneself.

ety: sudhī + Prk. डो or डओ = S. रः or लः, or र or ल+कः
def: adj. = su-dhī, q.v. s.f. Memory, &c. ( = sudh, q.v.). s.f. Consciousness, knowledge; sensation, perception; thought; memory, remembrace; attention, notice; care, regard; intelligence
subs: s.f. Consciousness and right understanding; sense; sensation, perception, care, &c. ( = sudh)
or sudh bisarnā, To lose (one's) consciousness, to lose (one's) senses
(-kī), To retain consciousness (of), to give (one's) mind or attention (to), to be thoughtful (of), to bear in mind, to care (for), to regard, to look (after)
(-kī), To take thought (of), to remember; to take care (of), to look (after); to inquire (into); to accommodate
adj. Devoid of consciousness, insensible; senseless; out of (one's) senses, beside oneself.

def: adj. Firm; considerate.

ety: sudhī + Prk. डो or डओ = S. रः or लः, or र or ल+कः
def: adj. = su-dhī, q.v. s.f. Memory, &c. ( = sudh, q.v.). s.f. Consciousness, knowledge; sensation, perception; thought; memory, remembrace; attention, notice; care, regard; intelligence
subs: s.f. Consciousness and right understanding; sense; sensation, perception, care, &c. ( = sudh)
or sudh bisarnā, To lose (one's) consciousness, to lose (one's) senses
(-kī), To retain consciousness (of), to give (one's) mind or attention (to), to be thoughtful (of), to bear in mind, to care (for), to regard, to look (after)
(-kī), To take thought (of), to remember; to take care (of), to look (after); to inquire (into); to accommodate
adj. Devoid of consciousness, insensible; senseless; out of (one's) senses, beside oneself.

def: v.t. To kindle, light, to cause to blaze; to inflame ( = sulgānā).
v.n. To be cleansed, to be purified (metal, &c.).

adj. (Braj) = sūdhā, q.v. s.f. The beverage of the gods, nectar; ambrosia; the nectar or honey of flowers; juice; water; — whitewash, plaster, mortar; a brick; — lightning; — adj. Comfortable, in easy circumstances, at ease, peaceable
subs: (˚dhā+āk˚), s.m. lit. 'Mine of nectar,' the moon (as the supposed repository of the nectar of the gods); moonlight
vulg. sudhāṅsu (˚dhā+aṅ˚), s.m. lit. 'Nectar-rayed,' the moon ( = sudhākar).

ety: sudhār(nā)+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं; — and āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: Forming, fashioning; regulation, management, correction, rectification, amendment, improvement, adjustment.

ety: sudhār(nā)+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं; — and āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: Forming, fashioning; regulation, management, correction, rectification, amendment, improvement, adjustment.
v.t. To cause to be reminded, to have (one) put in mind.
v.t. To cause to be cleaned or purified.
adj. Lent, or borrowed, at interest; at interest.
s.f. The light half of the lunar month (from the new to the full moon)
subs: s.f. The fourth day of the light half of the month.

def: s.f. corr. of sudī, q.v. s.f. The light half of the lunar month (from the new to the full moon)
subs: s.f. The fourth day of the light half of the month.

def: s.m. Fine day, fine weather; auspicious day, happy day; good or prosperous times.
ex: سدن میں موجود ہر ایک شخص کو خوش آمدید
Welcome to every member presented in the chamber.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit सदन (sadana), ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *sed-.

def: adj. See s.v. sūd. s.m. Utility, advantage, benefit; profit, gain; interest, usury
subs: s.m. The accumulation of interest so as to double the original debt
adj. Useful, beneficial, advantageous, profitable, lucrative; benefited, profited
(-ko), Advantage to accrue (to), to be benefited, to profit

def: s.m. (f. -a) = شودر śūdr, q.v.
s.m. A man of the fourth or servile caste of the Hindūs (whose only business, according to Manu, was to serve the three higher castes; he is fabled to have sprung from the feet of Brahmā).
s.f. A woman of the Śūdra caste; the wife of a Śūdra.
s.f. = شودراني śūdrāṇi, q.v.
s.f. = sūdrānī or śūdrānī, q.v.
s.f. A woman of the Śūdra caste; the wife of a Śūdra.
s.f. = śūdrāṇī, q.v.
s.f. = S شودرتو शूद्रत्व śūdratva, s.m. The nature or condition of a Śūdra; servility.
s.m. = S شودرتا शूद्रता śūdratā, s.f. The nature or condition of a Śūdra; servility.
s.f. = شودري śūdrī, q.v. (syn. sūdrānī).
s.f. = śūdrāṇī, q.v.
adj. & adv. Very hard, very firm, solid; — very firmly.
adj. Clearly visible; — good-looking, handsome, &c. ( = su-darśan).
s.m. pl. (of ṣadr, q.v.), Breasts; higher or upper parts; chiefs; ministers, &c.
(inf. n. of صدر 'to issue, proceed,' &c.), s.f.(?) Issuing, proceeding, emanating, going forth (from, -se); taking place, happening, &c.
adj. & s.m. Very wicked, abandoned, profligate; — an abandoned character, a profligate.
adj. Very difficult of attainment, quite unattainable, inaccessible; impracticable; very scarce, rare.
adj. Very difficult to be done, most arduous.

prep. = سو sū, q.v. insep. prefix [opp. to dus or dur, and ku; and corresponding to the Gr. εὖ), Good, well, excellent, excellently: neat, elegant, beautiful, beautifully; honourable, worthy of respect or reverence; excessive, excessively, exceedingly, much, very; readily, easily, willingly, quickly. (It is a particle which is prefixed to substantives almost ad libitum. Many words beginning with it will be found in Hindī literature which it has been deemed superfluous to insert in a dictionary.)
s.m. Wool; a piece of wool, or cloth, or thread, &c. (put into an (Indian) inkstand); — the cross-threads of gold-lace (goṭā); a kind of silk stuff
subs: (-meṅ), To put a piece of cloth, &c. (into an inkstand).

ety: sūf+ār, sūf = suf; rt. Zend śif = S. क्षिप्
def: s.m. & f. A small perforation; eye (of a needle); notch (of an arrow).

ety: for A. صقة, v.n. fr. صفّ 'to set or place in a row'
def: s.m. A kind of vestibule or portico for shade and shelter, open in front; a place for reclining on before the doors of eastern houses; — a sofa, a couch.
s.f. = ṣuffa, q.v. s.m. A kind of vestibule or portico for shade and shelter, open in front; a place for reclining on before the doors of eastern houses; — a sofa, a couch.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صِفَت (sifat), from Arabic صِفَة (ṣifa).

def: adj. Woollen; — wise, intelligent; pious, devout; — s.m. A ṣūfī or one of a peculiar sect of Moḥammadan devotees so called (they are said to be freethinkers or pantheists in matters of religion: outwardly they conform to the Moḥammadan creed, but are looked upon with great suspicion by the more orthodox. The name is probably derived from the Gr. σοφὸς; but some connect it with the ṣūfī or. woollen garments worn by this class of devotees); — a deist; — a dancing dervish; — an abstemious person.
s.m. The sect of Sūfīs.
s.m. pl. (of ṣūfī), Religious brethren of the sect of Sūfīs.
adj. Of or relating to the Sūfis; like a Sūfī; simple, plain.
s.f.(?) Settlings, sediment, dregs, lees, refuse.
s.m. Meanness (of origin); depression, lowness; — refuse; — adj. Inferior, mean, &c. ( = sifla, q.v.).
adj. Inferior, lower, infernal; mean.
s.m. = سفره sufra, q.v.

ety: for A. سفرة sufrat, v.n. fr. سفر; see safar
def: s.m. (orig.) 'The food of the traveller'; the receptacle for food; the thing (whatever it be) upon which one eats; a round leathern bag for holding food, so formed as to serve also for a table when spread out on the ground; a tablecloth, napkin; — the anus
subs: s.m. Remover or spreader of the sufra.

ety: perf. part. of suftan; rt. Zend śif = S. क्षिप्
def: part. Pierced, perforated, drilled, bored (as a pearl, &c.).
s.f. pl. (of ṣaff, q.v.), Ranks, rows, lines.
s.m. A parrot ( = suggā, q.v.). s.m. (f. -ī), A parrot.
